title: Missing Null Check in String Concatenation

statement.markdown: Implement a function that concatenates two strings. The function should handle two strings as input. It should return a new string which is the concatenation of the two input strings. Note that if the input strings are null, they should be handled correctly.
statement.outputFormat: A new string which is the concatenation of s1 and s2
statement.notes: Time complexity: O(n + m), where n and m are the lengths of the input strings

examples.explanation: Input string s1 is null, so we return s2.
Input strings s1 and s2 are not null.

hints.text: Handle the case where one of the input strings is null.

starterCode.java: public class Solution { public String solve(String s1, String s2) { // TODO: implement return null; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> str: # TODO: implement pass
starterCode.javascript: function solve(s1, s2) { // TODO: implement return '' }

buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public String solve(String s1, String s2) { if (s1.equals(s2)) { return s1 + s2; } else { return s2 + s1; } } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> str: if s1 == s2: return s1 + s2 else: return s2 + s1
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(s1, s2) { if (s1 == s2) { return s1 + s2; } else { return s2 + s1; } }

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, s1: str, s2: str) -> str:

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
